title: Will Evangelion 30 be released to American theaters
display_title: Will Evangelion 30 be released to American theaters
wikitext:
[]: Will Evangelion 3.0 be released to American theaters?             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Mon Aug 27, 2012 10:19 pm I have been meaning to ask this question for awhile because i never did find any legitimate information. But if 3.0 is released in America will it be released at few theaters?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Aug 27, 2012 11:38 pm I would assume so. 2.0 played in a very limited number of select theaters the week (or maybe even night) before it was released on Blu-ray/DVD.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Aug 28, 2012 2:38 am At some point. The last two entries did. But the engagements are very limited and you're probably shit out of luck if you don't live in or near a densely populated city. Knowing the way things are going now you'll probably have a better chance of getting Eva 3.0 ON DEMAND than see it in a theater. Regardless, this is all stuff that is at least a year and a half away. So there's no rush.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Aug 28, 2012 10:59 am God damn it FUNimation! Before 2.0 came out in Theaters here you were all like "Yeah, it's gonna in like a hundred theaters!" Then I realized you cut that into like twenty. I'm not gonna drive all the way to Fort Lauderdale just to see a movie. Come on, air at The Loop next time, every other Anime movie that gets released goes to The Loop, if not they at least go to Down Town Disney. Keep up FUNimation!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 28, 2012 12:10 pm It's been Funimation's practice to push for a theatrical run of their big theatrical licenses. I doubt 3.0 will be any different. The better question is will it even be worth it. It will probably go to select theaters after its been released to video.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Aug 28, 2012 12:21 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: It will probably go to select theaters after its been released to video.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Tue Aug 28, 2012 12:27 pm Well there's not just Funimation; Eleven Arts and Cinema Arts have their hand in the American release too, so I'm sure there's some contractual language that says the theatrical run has to precede the video release.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 28, 2012 12:31 pm JoeD80 wrote: Well there's not just Funimation; Eleven Arts and Cinema Arts have their hand in the American release too, so I'm sure there's some contractual language that says the theatrical run has to precede the video release.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Sat Sep 01, 2012 12:25 am If there is a release of 3.0 in NA theaters, it'll probably be very, very close to the actual release of the 3.0 (or will we just go straight to 3.3?) blu-ray. That said, living so close to NY has its advantages. I'd assume 3.0 is going to show in an NY theater.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Mon Sep 03, 2012 1:00 am TheFriskyIan wrote: God damn it FUNimation! Before 2.0 came out in Theaters here you were all like "Yeah, it's gonna in like a hundred theaters!" Then I realized you cut that into like twenty. I'm not gonna drive all the way to Fort Lauderdale just to see a movie.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Sep 03, 2012 1:26 am Let's see: Evangelion 1.0 was released in America; Evangelion 2.0 was released in America; I dunno about Evangelion 3.0 , you guys, but I think I'm beginning to see a pattern.

[]:             Quote Post by Draconis » Mon Sep 03, 2012 2:26 am gatotsu911 wrote: Let's see: Evangelion 1.0 was released in America; Evangelion 2.0 was released in America; I dunno about Evangelion 3.0 , you guys, but I think I'm beginning to see a pattern.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Sep 03, 2012 11:11 am Incidentally, the reason for the long-ass wait (two years seems to be the standard) for the Eva films isn't Funimation, it's Gainax/Khara. I don't know the specifics, but I think they wait a long time before even putting these movies on the market for US licensing companies because they know Eva is a huge property and they want to 1) prevent reverse importation as much as possible, and 2) wring as much money out of a prospective licensee as they can. That this basically gives the finger to US fans and is taken by many as a license to pirate the movies does not concern them. But it's not Funimation. You think they like losing sales because fans and potential customers have already seen a two-year-old film?

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Sep 03, 2012 11:45 am gatotsu911 wrote: But it's not Funimation. You think they like losing sales because fans and potential customers have already seen a two-year-old film?

[]:             Quote Post by Draconis » Mon Sep 03, 2012 11:47 am gatotsu911 wrote: Incidentally, the reason for the long-ass wait (two years seems to be the standard) for the Eva films isn't Funimation, it's Gainax/Khara. I don't know the specifics, but I think they wait a long time before even putting these movies on the market for US licensing companies because they know Eva is a huge property and they want to 1) prevent reverse importation as much as possible, and 2) wring as much money out of a prospective licensee as they can. That this basically gives the finger to US fans and is taken by many as a license to pirate the movies does not concern them. But it's not Funimation. You think they like losing sales because fans and potential customers have already seen a two-year-old film?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Sep 03, 2012 12:07 pm Yeah. The problem in the hold up has always been Gainax/Khara overestimating Eva's popularity in the rest of the world. The anime dubbing business is a pretty small market in the grand scheme of things & right now no one can contend with Funimation who can easily out buy everyone. The hold up has been Gainax/Khara holding out hope that the big distributors - Universal, 20th Century Fox, Warner Brothers, etc - would express interest distributing Evangelion in the western market. I'm guessing one of the producers was hoping for something similar to the Disney & Ghibli business relation. That's never going to happen. Anywho, I don't think Funimation getting the rights to 3.0 will take as long as before this time around. Gaianx/Khara tried twice to entice the big guys & they've never taken a bite of the apple so why should they now? Funimation though isn't without fault I'd say. On Dec 31 2008, a good 16 months after Evangelion 1.0's release, Funimation announced they had the rights to the first Rebuild film but they held the DVD release until late November 2009 so they could have their miniscule theatrical release. That always seemed like a curiosity to me since non-Disney dubs are usually super cheap affairs that can be done in literally two or three weeks. I've never looked into it but I feel Funimations decision to hold onto Eva 1.0 for almost a whole year was to make sure they turned in heavy profits in whatever business quarter the 1.0 release fell into. In regards to 3.0 getting a US release - DVD/Bluray - I'd place my bets on it coming out first quarter of 2014.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Tue Sep 04, 2012 8:24 am There's always the possibility of special screenings. I remember that when Evangelion 1.0 and 2.0 came out, the movie theater near my place made two single midnight screenings. (They had also done that for the Death Note movies). It's always worth keeping an eye open for special opportunities like this. ;P

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Tue Sep 04, 2012 12:34 pm Well that is possible but until then we will have to find out.

[]:             Quote Post by tmantran » Sat Sep 08, 2012 3:07 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: God damn it FUNimation! Before 2.0 came out in Theaters here you were all like "Yeah, it's gonna in like a hundred theaters!" Then I realized you cut that into like twenty. I'm not gonna drive all the way to Fort Lauderdale just to see a movie. Come on, air at The Loop next time, every other Anime movie that gets released goes to The Loop, if not they at least go to Down Town Disney. Keep up FUNimation!

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sat Sep 08, 2012 3:34 pm tmantran wrote: I presume you live in central Florida? I live in Orlando and went to the showing in Clearwater. Hopefully they'll do 3.0 too. Here's the theater's website, FYI: http://www.clearwatercinemacafe.com/. They showed the subbed version (which is what I prefer) and the dub version was shown a few months later at MegaCon.
